mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating household income and consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and business demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60d returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during a post-shock recovery phase reflect rapid capacity utilization and corporate earnings rebounds, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5d returns specifically during post-shock recovery periods, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate drop below 2.0% triggers aggressive liquidity provision and portfolio rebalancing into equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS crosses below 2.0%, or the signal generates a negative Sharpe ratio over a 1-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases wage inflation pressures and corporate operating costs, compressing profit margins and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases show no negative correlation with 60d forward returns, or the hypothesis yields a positive Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP combined with stable consumer price indices indicates robust economic expansion without inflationary constraints, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising GDPC1 and stable CPIAUCSL fails to predict positive 20d returns with a win rate above 55% in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals weakening manufacturing demand and impending earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansions do not precede negative 5d returns, or the predictive accuracy falls below 50% in a regime-filtered backtest.
